prompt_queue.prompt: Maintenance pass for non-technical, user-facing living documentation. Review source code, UI strings, commands, configuration, tests, examples, existing docs, recent issues, and recent pull requests for documentation gaps, drift, fragmentation, unclear user guidance, and documentation information architecture problems. When the runner provides a workspace handle, inspect and edit documentation through workspace tools, commit and push the scoped docs change, then open one documentation pull request. Preserve or improve the repository's existing docs taxonomy, index convention, and topic grouping; create audience folders such as user/ only when the repository already uses that convention or the consumer prompt explicitly asks for it. Prefer focused updates that keep existing docs aligned with current user-visible behavior: what users can do, getting started, setup, configuration, common tasks, permissions, compatibility, operational expectations, and troubleshooting. Use no_changes when existing user docs are accurate, sufficiently broad for the changed or inspected areas, and easy to navigate.
